metadata.summary: Python library for communicating with Delta Tau PMACs (both Turbo and Power)

documentation:
[![CI](https://github.com/DiamondLightSource/dls-pmac-lib/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/DiamondLightSource/dls-pmac-lib/actions/workflows/ci.yml)
[![Coverage](https://codecov.io/gh/DiamondLightSource/dls-pmac-lib/branch/main/graph/badge.svg)](https://codecov.io/gh/DiamondLightSource/dls-pmac-lib)
[![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/dls-pmaclib.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/dls-pmaclib)
[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-Apache%202.0-blue.svg)](https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0)

# dls_pmaclib

Python library for communicating with Delta Tau PMACs (both Turbo and Power)

This is where you should write a short paragraph that describes what your module does,
how it does it, and why people should use it.

Source          | <https://github.com/DiamondLightSource/dls-pmac-lib>
:---:           | :---:
PyPI            | `pip install dls-pmaclib`
Docker          | `docker run ghcr.io/diamondlightsource/dls-pmac-lib:latest`
Documentation   | <https://diamondlightsource.github.io/dls-pmac-lib>
Releases        | <https://github.com/DiamondLightSource/dls-pmac-lib/releases>

This is where you should put some images or code snippets that illustrate
some relevant examples. If it is a library then you might put some
introductory code here:

```python
from dls_pmaclib import __version__

print(f"Hello dls_pmaclib {__version__}")
```

Or if it is a commandline tool then you might put some example commands here:

```
python -m dls_pmaclib --version
```

<!-- README only content. Anything below this line won't be included in index.md -->

See https://diamondlightsource.github.io/dls-pmac-lib for more detailed documentation.

modules.name: dls_pmaclib
documentation:
Top level API.


Version number as calculated by https://github.com/pypa/setuptools_scm

modules.name: dls_pmaclib._version
documentation: file generated by setuptools_scm
don't change, don't track in version control

classes.name: dls_pmaclib.dls_pmcpreprocessor.ClsPmacParser

methods.name: dls_pmaclib.dls_pmcpreprocessor.ClsPmacParser.__init__
documentation:
Create an instance of the preprocessor.  Specify the paths
to search for include files using the 'includePaths' parameter
which should be a list of strings, each one specifying a
path (the current directory is automatically included in
the search).

methods.name: dls_pmaclib.dls_pmcpreprocessor.ClsPmacParser.parse
documentation:
Expand a 'pmc' file.  The 'pmcFileName' is opened and processed
into expanded text which is returned.  An map of predefined macro
expansions may be passed ('defines'), along with the booleans
'comments' and 'debug' which control the stripping of comments and
the insertion of simulation debug information respectively.

methods.name: dls_pmaclib.dls_pmcpreprocessor.ClsPmacParser.substitute_macros
documentation:
Expands any macros defined by 'macro_dict' in the given 'text'.
The expanded text is returned.

methods.name: dls_pmaclib.dls_pmcpreprocessor.ClsPmacParser.saveOutput
documentation: Writes the processed output to the specified file.

A class that pre-processes Delta-Tau 'pmc' files by making macro
substitutions and expanding included files (and nested include
files as deep as necessary).  It may optionally
strip comments and insert simulation debug information.

modules.name: dls_pmaclib.__main__
documentation: Interface for `python -m dls_pmaclib`.

functions.name: dls_pmaclib.__main__.main
documentation: Argument parser for the CLI.

classes.name: dls_pmaclib.dls_pmacremote.RemotePmacInterface

methods.name: dls_pmaclib.dls_pmacremote.RemotePmacInterface.connect
documentation: Connect to the host
Returns None if success. Error string if no connection parameters are
set or if failure.

methods.name: dls_pmaclib.dls_pmacremote.RemotePmacInterface.disconnect
documentation: disconnect from the host

methods.name: dls_pmaclib.dls_pmacremote.RemotePmacInterface.sendCommand
documentation:
Send a single command to the controller and block until a response from
the controller.
This is a return-format wrapper for the _sendCommand() method, written
as the existing code uses this
form of sendCommand() a lot.
Arguments: * command (str): is the command to be sent
            * shouldWait (bool, optional, default True): whether to
            wait
            on the semaphore.
              This should normally be left default. If we have acquired
              the semaphore manually,
              then specify shouldWait = False (and don't forget to
              release
              the semaphore eventually).
Returns: A tuple (response, wasSuccessful), where:
          * wasSuccessful (bool): is True unless there was an I/O
          problem
          during comm with the PMAC
                                  or the response does not have
                                  recognised
                                  terminators.
                                  Note that PMAC may still return an
                                  "ERRxxx" code; this function will
                                  still
                                  consider that a successful
                                  transmission.
          * response (str): is either a string returned by the PMAC (on
          success), or an error message (on failure)

methods.name: dls_pmaclib.dls_pmacremote.RemotePmacInterface._sendCommand
documentation: Send a single command to the controller and block until a response from
the controller.
This is the actual method that does the I/O with the remote PMAC,
and needs to be overridden in a child class;
each read/write cycle should be implemented to block.
Returns: a string with PMAC's response to the command.
Throws: an IOError if any I/O-related error occured.

methods.name: dls_pmaclib.dls_pmacremote.RemotePmacInterface.getPmacModelCode
documentation: Return a number designating which PMAC model this is, or Exception on
error.

methods.name: dls_pmaclib.dls_pmacremote.RemotePmacInterface.getPmacModel
documentation: Return a string designating which PMAC model this is, or None on error

methods.name: dls_pmaclib.dls_pmacremote.RemotePmacInterface._getNumberOfMacroStationAxes
documentation: Get the number of macro stations available. This is given by number of
Macro ICs available. One MACRO IC can control up to 8 axes,
so the number returned is a multiple of 8. The availability of MACRO
ICs is determined by querying I20..23 (read-only variables).
The first MACRO IC is available if I20 (MACRO IC base address) is
non-zero.
The second, third, and fourth MACRO IC are similarly addressed by I21,

methods.name: dls_pmaclib.dls_pmacremote.RemotePmacInterface.getNumberOfAxes
documentation: Get the total number of axes available

methods.name: dls_pmaclib.dls_pmacremote.RemotePmacInterface.getAxisMacroStationNumber
documentation: Returns: Macro station number
Raises: ValueError if asking for non-existent axis, or not a MACRO ring
axis

methods.name: dls_pmaclib.dls_pmacremote.RemotePmacInterface.isMacroStationAxis
documentation: Return True if this is a macro station axis, False if it is an
on-board-PMAC axis

methods.name: dls_pmaclib.dls_pmacremote.RemotePmacInterface.getIVars
documentation: Get a list of values of Ixxxx variables, where xxxx = base + offset (
for each offset).

methods.name: dls_pmaclib.dls_pmacremote.RemotePmacInterface.getAxisSetupIVars
documentation: Get a list of values of the "Setup I-variables" for a particular axis.
The "Setup I-variables" (thus named in the PMAC Software Reference
Manual) are the ones in the Ixxyy range, where xx = axis number,
yy = offset.
E.g. I one wants variables Ixx30, Ixx31 and Ixx32 for axis 14,
the should call self.getAxisISetupVars(14, [30,31,32]).

methods.name: dls_pmaclib.dls_pmacremote.RemotePmacInterface.getAxisMsIVars
documentation: Get macro station I-variables for a particular axis (or None on failure)

methods.name: dls_pmaclib.dls_pmacremote.RemotePmacInterface.getOnboardAxisI7000PlusVarsBase
documentation: Calculate the base I-variable number in the I70mn (say, "I7000+") range
for an onboard Geobrick axis.

methods.name: dls_pmaclib.dls_pmacremote.RemotePmacInterface.sendSeries
documentation: function that sends out a whole list of commands to the pmac
(like from a file...). The function waits for a response from each command
and registers any errors returned.
After a command has been sent and a response received, the function
will post an event
to update the progress dialog in the main (GUI) thread.
cmdLst is a list of tuples: (lineNumber, command)
where command is the command from the file and lineNumber
is the line number from the original file.

methods.name: dls_pmaclib.dls_pmacremote.RemotePmacInterface.commandNeedsDoubleTimeout
documentation: Controls whether to wait for double the normal timeout for this
message. Currently only used for the SAVE command.

methods.name: dls_pmaclib.dls_pmacremote.RemotePmacInterface.jogInc
documentation: Jog incrementally
\motor the motor number to jog.
\direction string either "pos" or "neg"
\distance in counts

methods.name: dls_pmaclib.dls_pmacremote.RemotePmacInterface.testGetAxisMacroStationNumber
documentation: A very basic test framework. Prints out test results onto standard output.
Run pmac.runTests() on a newly created pmac = PmacRemoteInterface(...)
object, which is already successfully connect()-ed.

This class provides a common interface to a remote PMAC. It provides
methods
       to connect to the PMAC (e.g. via a Telnet terminal server
   session or Ethernet), to disconnect, and to issue commands. It provides
       methods for some basic axis commands (e.g. move/jog axis etc.).
       It is
       a base class that should not be instantiated directly.
noinspection PyPep8Naming

methods.name: dls_pmaclib.dls_pmacremote.PPmacSshInterface.wait_to_receive_string
documentation: Wait until a specified string is seen within
received data before proceeding

methods.name: dls_pmaclib.dls_pmacremote.PPmacSshInterface.getPmacModel
documentation: Return a string designating which PMAC model this is, or None on error

methods.name: dls_pmaclib.dls_pmacremote.PPmacSshInterface.getPmacModelCode
documentation: Return a number designating which PMAC model this is, or Exception on
error.

methods.name: dls_pmaclib.dls_pmacremote.PPmacSshInterface.getNumberOfAxes
documentation: Get the total number of axes available

methods.name: dls_pmaclib.dls_pmacremote.PPmacSshInterface.getFile
documentation: Copy remote file to local host

methods.name: dls_pmaclib.dls_pmacremote.PPmacSshInterface.putFile
documentation: Copy local file to remote host

methods.name: dls_pmaclib.dls_pmacremote.PPmacSshInterface.sendSshCommand
documentation: Send a command via ssh (not gpascii)

classes.name: dls_pmaclib.dls_pmacremote.PmacEthernetInterface

methods.name: dls_pmaclib.dls_pmacremote.PmacEthernetInterface.connect
documentation: Attempts to open a connection to a remote PMAC.
Returns None on success, or an error message string on failure.

methods.name: dls_pmaclib.dls_pmacremote.PmacEthernetInterface.disconnect
documentation: Disconnect from the telnet session
Returns None on success; error message on failure.
Allows connection to a PMAC over an Ethernet interface.
noinspection PyPep8Naming

classes.name: dls_pmaclib.dls_pmacremote.PmacTelnetInterface

methods.name: dls_pmaclib.dls_pmacremote.PmacTelnetInterface.connect
documentation: connect to the telnet session.
Returns None if success. Error string if no connection parameters are
set or if failure.

methods.name: dls_pmaclib.dls_pmacremote.PmacTelnetInterface.disconnect
documentation: disconnect from the telnet session

methods.name: dls_pmaclib.dls_pmacremote.PmacTelnetInterface._sendCommand
documentation: Send a single telnet command to the controller and wait for
the expected result returned by the controler.

Allows connection to a PMAC using a Telnet connection to a terminal
server session.

classes.name: dls_pmaclib.dls_pmacremote.PmacSerialInterface

methods.name: dls_pmaclib.dls_pmacremote.PmacSerialInterface.connect
documentation: Connect here
connect to the telnet session.
Returns None if success. Error string if no connection parameters are
set or if failure.

methods.name: dls_pmaclib.dls_pmacremote.PmacSerialInterface.disconnect
documentation: disconnect from the telnet session

methods.name: dls_pmaclib.dls_pmacremote.PmacSerialInterface._sendCommand
documentation: Send a single telnet command to the controller and wait for
the expected result returned by the controler.
Allows connection to a PMAC via RS232.
